title: Close Tier C / singleton / risk-class self-authorization loopholes (G-07)

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Design-only lane (spec-first, matching this repo's existing precedent for this class of change). No code, workflow, or hook file was modified in this revision. This is the last commit on this branch containing reviewed design/proof content prior to this trailing evidence-rebind commit itself; this trailing commit only updates SHA-binding fields, verifier identity, and row-count freshness in this file and verification.md -- no diff-summary.md content change. Verified via `git merge-base --is-ancestor 0bd318056e14fb765b639741065582a8dd5007a1 <actual-PR-head>` at push time (see verification.md for the literal command output). This SHA supersedes the prior binding (fc538cc530cfc0af72d0d5e78665b6b42f32a4ba), which was itself superseded because a further-corrected review round required rewording an earlier commit message (content-preserving; see verification.md) to fix a real branch-discipline failure, which changed that commit's hash without changing its tree.

verifier.note: Genuinely distinct from manifest.created_by ("claude"): this is GitHub's Codex automated-review bot, which independently reviewed two exact PR heads on this branch (7f5c4d6e9b23193c7cf24ab94e50e37fafa196cf and 2d39b593b266f780a41f28a4855278136554bca9 before this rewording), catching one real finding each time (a proof-SHA-ancestry concern -- addressed by the rebind above -- and, on the earlier head, a genuine self-verification loophole in this very field, since fixed). This is real, independent, automated review, not the same identity as the implementer -- but it is explicitly NOT a substitute for this repo's actual T1 closure authority: the `t1-approved` label and a Griff-authored `pm-verdict/v1` APPROVED comment remain separately required before merge, and neither exists as of this revision. `independent_owner_certifier` stays false because Codex is not the human PM owner-certifier this repo's T1 policy requires.

scope.note: docs/06_status/proof/UTV2-1451/diff-summary.md and this evidence bundle are the only substantive content; docs/06_status/lanes/UTV2-1451.json and .ops/sync/UTV2-1451.yml are lane bookkeeping.

runtime_proof.note: This lane's own diff is docs-only, but T1 tier requires showing the baseline live-DB suite is green against real infrastructure rather than declaring runtime proof not applicable -- corrected from an earlier revision that incorrectly marked this inapplicable.

queries.description: database-smoke.test.ts: insert + delete a real submission row via the repository bundle (persist a submission and settlement when Supabase is configured)
database-smoke.test.ts: insert + delete a real pick row tied to the submission under test

database-smoke.test.ts: lifecycle-event rollback assertions across the enqueue/delivery/settlement invalid-atomic-write tests
database-smoke.test.ts: audit-row rollback assertions across the invalid atomic-write tests
database-smoke.test.ts: re-settlement correction-chain tests (correction row created additively, original row not mutated)
database-smoke.test.ts: no-duplicate-participants assertion for a given external_id and sport

runtime_proof.row_counts_note: Monitored-table row counts captured via a direct SQL count(*) against the live project immediately after the pnpm test:db run recorded above; test rows are inserted and deleted within each test case (see queries above), so these counts reflect steady-state table volume, not test-added rows.

r_level.note: Docs-only diff; no R-level artifacts expected. R1/R2 runtime-proof fields above are populated regardless, since ops:truth-check's addUnsupportedRuntimeChecks requires them unconditionally for T1 tier.

known_gaps: Implementation of the two designed fixes (Tier C authorization artifact + gate; singleton-approval real record) is NOT part of this revision. Per PM verdict, a concrete, T1-tier, dispatch-ready blocking implementation child issue has been created and linked -- see diff-summary.md for its ID and acceptance criteria. This lane does not close the named loopholes; it is design + a PM-sanctioned deferred implementation via that linked child.

The mechanical tier-classifier blocking-phase cutover and its prerequisite baseline/sweep report are explicitly out of scope for this lane -- both require their own separate PM approval per the locked MECHANICAL_TIER_CLASSIFIER_SPEC.md.

The corrected PreToolUse notice mechanism (hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext + systemMessage on exit 0) is verified against the official Claude Code hooks reference and in-repo precedent from other (non-PreToolUse) hooks already using the systemMessage pattern, but was not empirically instrumented against a live PreToolUse invocation from within this design-only lane -- confidence is HIGH, not certain. diff-summary.md now requires the implementation lane (UTV2-1570) to ship an integration test or captured behavior proof of the actual PreToolUse payload surfacing, rather than relying on this design's confidence level as sufficient proof on its own.
